definition: Whether a policy uncertainty episode involves surprise or telegraphed shifts is the key variable determining whether buying rates vol pays off. In episodes where realized rates vol exceeded implied (QE1: -2.3 pts, taper tantrum: -1.1 pts, COVID: -4.1 pts), the shift was a surprise. Current fiscal-monetary risks are structural and well-discussed, tilting toward telegraphed and reducing expected long rates vol payoff.
evidence: Base rate for buying rates vol is 50/50 across 6 comparable episodes overall
Pair_0 debate rated this as sharpest analytical contribution
Transforms vague underpricing thesis into testable proposition
open_questions: Can telegraphed risks still produce surprises in timing or magnitude?
Is the current fiscal situation truly telegraphed or are markets underestimating tail scenarios?
How to operationalize the surprise/telegraphed classification in real time?
